excerpt: When the beneficiary learns something new from his benefactor, he should confess it with his tongue and confirm it with his heart, so that the knowledge he has heard with his ear will be absorbed in his thoughts.
source_location_hint: Instruction on the pedagogy of esoteric knowledge

excerpt: The more knowledge the angels acquire about Allah, the more their fear and longing increases.
source_location_hint: Discussion of the degrees of knowledge in angelic intellects

excerpt: Spiritual excellence is that you worship Allah as though you see Him; and if you cannot see Him, know that He sees you.
source_location_hint: Hadith regarding ihsan transmitted in the tradition of the Prophet

excerpt: The beneficiary must confirm not only the knowledge but also the conveyor and the station from which it is received.
Whoever fulfills his obligation receives fulfillment in return.
source_location_hint: Sayings of Imam Ali on reciprocal fulfillment

excerpt: A well-posed question is half the answer in itself.
source_location_hint: Traditions on the virtue of questioning

excerpt: Ask questions, ask questions, until people begin to say of you that you have lost your mind.
source_location_hint: Exhortation on the necessity of inquiry

excerpt: Without knowledge the animal powers of the soul become weak, and its carnal and irascible faculties awaken, rendering it susceptible to sin.
source_location_hint: On the spiritual effects of knowledge and its absence

excerpt: They seek to deceive Allah and those who believe, but they deceive only themselves, and they are not aware.
source_location_hint: Exegesis of Qur'anic verse on self-deception

excerpt: The beneficiary's question arises from the stirring of the Imam, and the mentor's answer comes through the enabling grace of the Imam. All praise returns to the Imam.
source_location_hint: On the role of the Imam as the source of knowledge transmission

excerpt: The faculty of imagination, like a mirror, possesses the ability to absorb forms and integrate them into itself, becoming able to encompass many concepts simultaneously.
source_location_hint: On the cosmic nature of the faculty of imagination

excerpt: The beneficiary, through successive mentors at each level, develops until he himself becomes able to guide others, transforming from one who receives to one who gives.
source_location_hint: On the perfection of the beneficiary's ascent through knowledge
